Claude Code is a command-line tool that lets you invoke AI directly from your terminal, but it's far more than a simple chat interface. It can split tasks, run multiple agents in parallel, and keep processing in the background — agent features that fundamentally change how you develop.

This article breaks down Claude Code's agent capabilities into three layers: sub-agents (task delegation), background tasks (async execution), and Agent Teams (multi-session coordination).

What Are Sub-Agents?

Sub-agents are specialized agents that handle specific tasks delegated from your main Claude Code session. Each runs in its own context window with independent permissions and tool access.

Why Sub-Agents Matter

Claude Code's context window is finite. On a large project, code exploration, test execution, and documentation searches eat into that context, leaving less room for the actual work at hand.

Sub-agents solve this problem. When you delegate investigation or search tasks to a sub-agent, all the trial-and-error and verbose output stays within that agent's context — never polluting your main conversation history. You preserve main session context while tackling complex tasks in parallel.

Built-in Sub-Agents

Claude Code comes with several pre-built sub-agents.

Explore agent. A fast, read-only agent specialized for codebase exploration. It handles file searches, code searches, and structural analysis. Claude automatically invokes it when it needs to read and understand code.

Plan agent. Used for drafting implementation plans. It explores the codebase, identifies relevant files, considers architectural trade-offs, and returns step-by-step plans.

General-purpose agent. Handles complex research and multi-step tasks with access to a wide range of tools including code search, file reading, and web search.

Creating Custom Sub-Agents

When your project has specific work patterns, you can create custom sub-agents. Use the /agents command or manually create a Markdown file with YAML frontmatter.

The tools field restricts which tools the agent can use. A test runner might only need Bash, Read, and Grep — no need for Write or Edit. Granting minimal permissions prevents unintended changes.

The model field lets you choose which model powers the agent. Use Haiku for fast investigation tasks, Opus for complex reasoning — matching model capability to task requirements.

Practical Pattern: Think with Opus, Execute with Sonnet

A cost-effective pattern is to run your main session on Opus for high-level design and judgment, while delegating actual code generation and test execution to Sonnet sub-agents.

[Opus] Design, review, and decision-making
  ├── [Sonnet] Frontend implementation
  ├── [Sonnet] API endpoint implementation
  └── [Sonnet] Test creation and execution

Opus provides superior architectural judgment while Sonnet handles implementation details at higher speed and lower cost — optimizing both quality and efficiency.

Background Tasks

Basic Usage

Press Ctrl+B while a task is running in Claude Code to move it to the background. Your main session frees up immediately, letting you work on something else.

This is particularly powerful for time-consuming operations like builds and test runs. While tests are running in the background, you can edit other files, design the next feature, or review earlier work.

Async Sub-Agent Execution

When you spawn sub-agents, they inherently run in the background. You can launch multiple sub-agents simultaneously and collect their results later.

For example, during a refactoring task:

  1. Sub-agent A: Identify and fix affected tests
  2. Sub-agent B: Update documentation
  3. Sub-agent C: Verify type definition consistency

These run in parallel. Once all complete, your main session integrates the results.

Monitoring Tasks

You can check the status of background tasks at any time — pull in results from completed tasks or check progress on those still running.

Agent Teams (Experimental)

Overview

Released in February 2026, Agent Teams is Claude Code's most ambitious agent feature. It orchestrates multiple Claude Code sessions working together as a team on large-scale tasks.

How They Differ from Sub-Agents

The key difference between sub-agents and Agent Teams is communication.

Sub-agents operate within a single session and can only report results back to the main agent. They can't message each other, share mid-task discoveries, or coordinate without the main agent as intermediary.

Agent Teams removes that bottleneck entirely. One session acts as the team lead, while other sessions (teammates) work independently in their own context windows — and can communicate directly with each other.

When to Use Them

Agent Teams are especially effective for:

Large-scale refactoring. Assign frontend, backend, and testing responsibilities to separate sessions. Each teammate works in parallel while sharing progress updates that inform the others' decisions.

Full-stack feature development. API design, database schema, and UI components each get their own teammate, sharing interface changes in real time as the feature takes shape.

Code review and fixes. One teammate reviews while another implements fixes immediately — a pair-programming workflow where both sides are AI-powered.

Things to Keep in Mind

Agent Teams is currently an experimental feature. Success depends on clear instructions to the team lead and well-defined roles for each teammate. Vague instructions can lead to overlapping work or conflicting changes.

Context Management Best Practices

Getting the most out of agent features requires thoughtful context management.

Use CLAUDE.md

Place a CLAUDE.md file at your project root to provide project-specific information to Claude. Coding conventions, architecture overviews, frequently used commands — all sessions including sub-agents will reference this file.

Mind Your Task Granularity

Tasks delegated to sub-agents should be self-contained. If a task depends on another task's output, you can't benefit from parallel execution.

Good decomposition:

  • Refactor file A (self-contained)
  • Add tests (self-contained)
  • Update documentation (self-contained)

Poor decomposition:

  • Change API type definitions (other tasks depend on this)
  • Fix components using the API (waiting on above)

Choose Models by Task

Not every task needs the most powerful model.

  • Opus: Architecture design, complex judgment, final review
  • Sonnet: General coding, test creation, documentation
  • Haiku: Simple searches, file reading, routine transformations

Wrapping Up

Claude Code's agent features bring a "team development" experience to solo developers.

Sub-agents conserve context. Background tasks eliminate wait times. Agent Teams tackle large-scale work. Combined, these features let a single developer achieve remarkable velocity.

Start with the built-in sub-agents, then create custom ones as you get comfortable. When you teach agents your project's specific patterns, Claude Code evolves from a simple tool into a teammate that deeply understands your codebase.

en.title: Practical Techniques for Automating Repetitive Tasks with Cowork
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: Learn how to combine Skills and Scheduled Tasks to dramatically streamline your daily workflows in Claude Desktop's Cowork mode.
en.content:
Practical Techniques for Automating Repetitive Tasks with Cowork

Cowork mode in Claude Desktop, announced in January 2026 as a research preview, extends Claude Code's agentic capabilities beyond coding into general knowledge work. Document creation, file organization, research synthesis — you can describe the outcome, step away, and come back to finished work.

This article focuses on two features that are especially powerful in practice: Skills and Scheduled Tasks. Together, they let you automate the repetitive work that eats into your day.

What Is Cowork Mode?

Cowork is essentially a digital coworker running inside Claude Desktop. Unlike standard chat, it offers several key capabilities.

Direct local file access. Select a folder and Claude can read and write files within it. No manual uploading or downloading required.

Sub-agent parallel processing. Complex work is broken into smaller tasks that run concurrently. Bulk file processing and cross-reference research complete much faster.

Professional deliverables. Excel spreadsheets with working formulas, formatted PowerPoint presentations, polished Word documents — output that's ready to submit as-is.

Long-running task support. Unlike standard conversation sessions, Cowork handles extended, complex processing without interruption.

Skills — Teaching Claude How to Work

What Are Skills?

Skills are a mechanism for teaching Claude repeatable workflows. You define best practices for a specific task type in a SKILL.md file, and Claude follows those rules when it works. When Claude recognizes what kind of work you need, it automatically loads the relevant skill file before starting.

For example, there are pre-built skills for Word documents (docx), presentations (pptx), spreadsheets (xlsx), and PDF processing (pdf).

Pre-installed Key Skills

Cowork ships with several skills out of the box.

Document skills. The core set includes docx (Word), pptx (presentations), xlsx (spreadsheets), and pdf (PDF processing). Each supports creating, reading, and editing files.

Frontend design. The frontend-design skill generates polished HTML/CSS/React components. Great for landing pages and dashboards.

SEO audit. The seo-audit skill diagnoses technical SEO issues on your website and suggests improvements.

Skill finder. Can't find what you need? The find-skills skill searches for installable skills. Just ask "Is there a skill that can do X?" and it will look for you.

Creating Custom Skills

When existing skills aren't enough, you can create your own. Use the skill-creator skill, or manually create a folder structure like this:

skills/
  my-custom-skill/
    SKILL.md

Inside SKILL.md, describe how you want Claude to behave in natural language. Three key elements to include:

Clear trigger conditions. Specify what keywords or contexts should activate this skill. For example: "When the user says 'generate report'" or "When a .csv file is attached."

Step-by-step procedures. Be specific rather than vague. "First read the data, then aggregate it, finally generate a chart" is much better than "process the data."

Output format specifications. Define the file format, naming conventions, and save locations for deliverables.

Scheduled Tasks — Set It and Forget It

Overview

The Scheduled Tasks feature, added in 2026, takes Cowork's automation to the next level. Describe a task once, set the frequency, and Cowork runs it automatically.

Daily, weekly, weekdays only, hourly, or on-demand — flexible scheduling is available. Each scheduled task launches as an independent Cowork session with access to all your connected tools, plugins, and MCP servers.

How to Set Up

There are two ways to create a scheduled task.

From chat. Type /schedule in any task session and Claude will guide you through setup interactively. It asks for the task name, description, prompt, and frequency step by step.

From the sidebar. Click "Scheduled" in the sidebar and create a new task manually. You can specify the name, description, prompt, frequency, model, and working folder.

Cron Expression Basics

Schedule frequency is specified using cron expressions. Here are some common patterns:

0 9 * * *       Every day at 9:00 AM
0 9 * * 1-5     Weekdays at 9:00 AM
30 8 * * 1      Every Monday at 8:30 AM
0 0 1 * *       First day of every month at midnight
0 */3 * * *     Every 3 hours

The five fields represent "minute hour day month weekday" in order. * means "all," 1-5 means "1 through 5," and */3 means "every 3." The timezone uses your machine's local time, so no UTC conversion is needed.

Practical Examples

Daily report generation. Every morning at 9 AM, aggregate data files in a specific folder and generate a summary report. Useful for daily sales summaries or project progress updates.

Periodic file organization. Once a week, sort files in your Downloads folder into subfolders by type. Perfect for anyone whose desktop or downloads folder tends to get cluttered.

Content monitoring. Periodically check your website's content and list any areas that need updating. Convenient for blog and documentation site maintenance.

Backup and archiving. Create regular backups of important documents and organize them into date-stamped archive folders.

Plugins — Extending Skills Further

The plugin system, released in late January 2026, bundles skills, connectors, slash commands, and sub-agents into a single package.

Pre-built Plugins

Eleven open-source plugins were available at launch, covering key business areas including productivity, enterprise search, marketing, and sales.

Installing Plugins

Install from the Cowork settings screen, or upload custom plugins. You can also use the Plugin Create plugin to build new plugins through an interactive conversation.

Connecting External Services with Connectors

Cowork connects to various external services through MCP (Model Context Protocol).

Google. Gmail and Google Drive integration for checking email and reading/writing documents.

Microsoft. Microsoft 365 integration for cross-application workflows between Excel and PowerPoint.

Project management. Integration with team tools like Asana, Slack, and Notion.

Design tools. Canva integration lets you create, edit, and export designs entirely from chat.

Add connectors by asking Claude within a Cowork session, or through the "Customize" section in settings.

Five Tips for Getting the Most Out of Cowork

1. Be Specific with Instructions

Instead of "make it look nice," try "A4 portrait, headings up to 3 levels, use Helvetica font." Claude handles vague instructions reasonably well, but specificity gets you closer to what you actually want.

2. Organize Your Folder Structure First

When the files in your working folder are well-organized, Claude's processing accuracy improves. Even just separating input files from output destinations makes a difference.

3. Iterate on Your Skills

You don't need a perfect skill from day one. Use it, tweak the SKILL.md, and gradually improve accuracy. This iterative approach is the most practical.

4. Start Small with Scheduled Tasks

Don't build complex automations right away. Start with something simple — like listing files in a folder — verify it works, then expand from there.

5. Make Preview a Habit

For tasks involving file editing or deletion, add a preview or confirmation step before execution. Cowork has safety measures built in, but developing the habit of checking with your own eyes is valuable.

Wrapping Up

By combining Cowork's Skills and Scheduled Tasks, you can automate many of the repetitive tasks you've been doing manually. The initial setup takes some time, but once the system is in place, you'll see significant time savings every day.

Pick one repetitive task, hand it off to Cowork, and see what happens. You might be surprised at how much it can do.

Cowork is currently available as a research preview on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans. Access it through the Claude Desktop app on macOS and Windows.

en.title: Rork Max Complete Guide — Build Native Swift Apps with AI
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: A comprehensive guide to building native Swift apps with Rork Max. Covers Apple Watch, Vision Pro, 2-click App Store publishing, and Claude Code integration.
en.content:
What Is Rork Max?

Rork Max is an AI-powered native Swift app builder launched in February 2026. Simply type a text prompt, and Rork Max generates a native Swift / SwiftUI application.

While the original Rork built cross-platform apps with React Native, Rork Max focuses exclusively on the Apple ecosystem. It's the only AI builder generating native Swift apps, unlocking platform capabilities like AR, Metal graphics, and widgets that React Native cannot access.

ℹ️
**Rork Max Highlights:** - The only AI builder generating native Swift apps - Entirely browser-based — no Xcode or Mac required - Hit $1.5M ARR within 3 days of launch - A working AR-enabled Pokémon Go clone was built from a single prompt

Supported Platforms

Rork Max supports every major platform in the Apple ecosystem.

iPhone / iPad (iOS / iPadOS)

The primary target. SwiftUI-based UI with layouts automatically optimized for both iPhone and iPad.

Apple Watch (watchOS)

Generate health apps, notification apps, and companion apps for watchOS. Native HealthKit integration is built in.

Apple TV (tvOS)

Build media apps, games, and streaming interfaces for the big screen. Focus Engine navigation is automatically implemented for tvOS.

Vision Pro (visionOS)

Generate spatial computing apps. Build 3D object placement, immersive AR experiences, and window-based interfaces from prompts alone.

iMessage Apps

Create sticker packs, mini-games, and interactive content that runs within iMessage.

Native API Access

Rork Max's greatest strength is full access to Apple platform native APIs.

ARKit / LiDAR Scanning

Prompt example:
"Create a furniture placement app that uses AR to preview
furniture in a room. Use LiDAR to scan the room and place
3D models realistically."

Metal Graphics

Prompt example:
"Create a 3D flight simulator using Metal for smooth
3D rendering with realistic terrain."

Home Screen Widgets / Dynamic Island / Live Activities

Prompt example:
"Create a weather app with Home Screen widgets, Dynamic
Island temperature display, and Live Activities for
weather alerts."

Siri Intents

Prompt example:
"Create a task manager. When I say 'Add a task' to Siri,
it should add a new task to the app."

HealthKit / HomeKit / NFC

  • HealthKit: Access steps, heart rate, sleep data
  • HomeKit: Control smart home devices
  • NFC: Read and write NFC tags

Core ML

Prompt example:
"Create an app that recognizes food from photos and
estimates calories using Core ML for on-device inference."

App Clips

Build lightweight app experiences that launch instantly from QR codes or NFC tags without installation.

2-Click App Store Publishing

Rork Max's most revolutionary feature is completing App Store publishing entirely in the browser.

Traditional Workflow

  1. Buy a Mac ($1,000+)
  2. Install Xcode (several GB)
  3. Register for Apple Developer Program ($99/year)
  4. Write code
  5. Configure Provisioning Profiles and Certificates
  6. Archive and upload to App Store Connect
  7. Wait for review

Rork Max Workflow

  1. Type a prompt in your browser
  2. Cloud Mac compiles natively
  3. Preview in streaming simulator (with real-time touch input)
  4. Install on device via QR code for testing
  5. Submit to App Store in 2 clicks
⚠️
**Apple Developer Program required:** You still need an Apple Developer Program membership ($99/year) for App Store publishing. However, Xcode and Mac ownership are not required.

Pricing

| Plan | Price | Features | |------|-------|----------| | Free | $0 | ~5 prompts/week (trial) | | Max | $200/month | Unlimited native Swift builds, all platforms |

Start with the Free plan, then upgrade to Max for serious development.

Rork Max × Claude Code Integration

Refine Rork Max-generated apps further with Claude Code.

Export and Setup

What Claude Code Can Improve

Practical Workflow

Step 1: Idea to Prototype (30 min)

"Create a running tracker app:
- GPS route recording
- HealthKit heart rate integration
- Apple Watch companion app
- Home Screen widget showing weekly distance
- Dark mode support"

Step 2: Simulator Testing (15 min)

Test in the streaming simulator with real-time touch input.

Step 3: Device Testing (15 min)

Scan QR code to install on iPhone. Test GPS and sensor features on real hardware.

Step 4: Claude Code Refinement (2-3 hours)

Export and refine with Claude Code. Add business logic, tests, and optimizations.

Step 5: App Store Publishing (30 min)

Submit via Rork Max's 2-click publishing. Review typically takes 24-48 hours.

ℹ️
**Total time:** From idea to App Store submission in approximately half a day.

Rork (React Native) vs Rork Max (Swift)

| Feature | Rork (React Native) | Rork Max (Swift) | |---------|--------------------|--------------------| | Platforms | iOS + Android | Apple only (iPhone, iPad, Watch, TV, Vision Pro) | | Language | JavaScript / TypeScript | Swift / SwiftUI | | Native features | Limited | Full access (ARKit, Metal, HealthKit, etc.) | | Widgets | No | Yes | | Dynamic Island | No | Yes | | Vision Pro | No | Yes | | App Store publishing | Via Expo | 2-click | | Pricing | Free–$30/mo | Free–$200/mo | | Android support | Yes | No |

How to choose: Need Android users? Use Rork (React Native). Going all-in on Apple with native features? Choose Rork Max.

Troubleshooting

Generated app doesn't match expectations

Make prompts more specific. Include screen layouts, data models, and Apple frameworks to use.

Compile errors

Rork Max compiles on cloud Macs automatically, so errors are rare. For complex feature combinations, split prompts and add features incrementally.

App Store rejection

Check Apple guidelines compliance with Claude Code:

Summary

Rork Max dramatically lowers the barrier to native Swift app development. Even without programming experience, you can build apps utilizing ARKit, Metal, and HealthKit from text prompts alone, and publish them to the App Store.

Combined with Claude Code, the "AI × AI" workflow takes generated code to production quality with testing and optimization — all in a single day.

FAQ

Do I need a Mac to use Rork Max?

No. Everything runs in the browser. Compilation happens on cloud Macs, so you can use Windows or Linux.

Can I build Android apps?

Rork Max is Apple-platform exclusive. For Android, use the React Native-based original Rork.

What can I do with the free plan?

Approximately 5 prompts per week for trial purposes. Serious development requires the Max plan ($200/month).

Can I use Rork Max without Claude Code?

Yes. Rork Max handles everything from generation to App Store publishing. However, Claude Code is strongly recommended for adding business logic and improving code quality.

Can it really build Vision Pro apps?

Yes. Rork Max generates visionOS spatial computing apps with 3D object placement, immersive experiences, and window-based interfaces.

en.title: Canva × Claude Integration Guide — Create and Edit Designs from AI Chat
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: Learn how to use the Canva MCP connector to create, search, edit, and export designs directly from Claude. Includes brand kit integration and template autofill.
en.content:
What Is Canva × Claude?

In January 2026, Canva launched its AI connector for Claude, enabling users to create, search, edit, and export Canva designs directly from Claude's chat interface.

This integration is powered by MCP (Model Context Protocol), allowing Claude to securely access your Canva account and manage design operations on your behalf.

ℹ️
**What the Canva MCP Connector Can Do:** - Generate new designs from text prompts - Search and browse existing designs - Edit text and images - Apply brand kits for on-brand designs - Autofill templates with data - Export to PDF, PNG, PPTX, and more - Manage and organize folders

Setup

Connecting on Claude.ai

  1. Log in to Claude.ai
  2. Click the connector icon in the chat input area
  3. Select Canva
  4. Log in with your Canva account and grant access

Once connected, you can access Canva features directly from Claude's chat.

Connecting on Cowork

The Canva connector is also available in Cowork. Add it from the settings panel and link your Canva account.

Basic Usage

Creating New Designs

Simply describe what you want in natural language.

"Create a 10-page presentation for a tech company
using blue and white colors. The topic is AI use cases."

Claude invokes Canva's design generation AI to create the presentation. You'll see design candidates to choose from, and your selection is saved to your Canva account.

Searching Existing Designs

"Find the marketing materials I created last week"

Claude searches your Canva account and displays matching designs.

Editing Designs

"Change the title on page 3 of this presentation
to 'Q1 Performance Report'"

Text replacement, image swaps, and formatting changes — all from chat instructions.

Exporting

"Export this design as a PDF"

Supports PDF, PNG, JPG, PPTX, GIF, and MP4 formats.

Brand Kit Integration

One of the most powerful aspects of this integration is access to Canva's brand kits directly from Claude.

What Are Brand Kits?

Canva brand kits store your brand colors, fonts, logos, and other brand assets in one place.

Generating On-Brand Designs

"Using our brand kit, create an Instagram post
announcing our new product release"

Claude displays available brand kits, lets you select one, then generates designs that follow your brand guidelines.

💡
**Brand consistency:** Specifying a brand kit ensures colors, fonts, and logo placement automatically conform to guidelines. Even when team members create designs separately, output remains consistent.

Template Usage

Searching Brand Templates

"Find a monthly report template"

Searches and displays brand templates saved in your Canva account.

Template Autofill

Automatically populate templates with data.

"Fill this template with this month's sales data:
Revenue: $120K, MoM growth: +15%, Target achievement: 108%"

Works with templates containing charts and graphs too.

Practical Workflows

Workflow 1: Weekly Report Automation

  1. Ask Claude to "create a report summarizing last week's project progress"
  2. Claude selects a brand template
  3. Generates the design based on information provided in chat
  4. Review and adjust content
  5. Export as PDF for sharing

Workflow 2: Batch SNS Content Creation

  1. Ask "create 5 social media posts for next week about our new feature release"
  2. Claude generates designs for Instagram, Twitter, and Facebook
  3. Adjust text and images for each
  4. Export as PNG

Workflow 3: Presentation Creation and Refinement

  1. Ask "create a presentation for next week's board meeting"
  2. Claude proposes an outline, generates design after approval
  3. Fine-tune: "change the chart on page 5 from bar to pie chart"
  4. Export as PPTX for final adjustments in PowerPoint

Page Operations

Reordering and Deleting Pages

"Swap pages 3 and 5"
"Delete the last 2 pages"

Merging Multiple Designs

"Combine these two presentations into one"

Combine pages from different designs into a new unified design.

Comments

Adding Comments to Designs

"Add a comment to this design: 'Please reconsider the logo placement'"

Reviewing and Replying to Comments

"Show me the comments on this design"

View unresolved comments and reply directly through Claude.

Supported Design Types

Canva × Claude supports a wide range of design types:

  • Presentations: Slide decks, pitch decks
  • Social Media: Instagram posts, Twitter/X posts, Facebook covers, YouTube thumbnails
  • Documents: Reports, proposals, memos
  • Marketing: Flyers, posters, business cards, invitations
  • Others: Logos, infographics, wallpapers, photo collages

Tips and Best Practices

Always Review

Always review AI-generated designs before publishing. Check for text errors and subtle deviations from brand guidelines.

Be Specific

Specific instructions produce better results than vague ones.

Before: "Make a nice poster"

After: "Create a portrait A3 poster with a blue gradient background and white text reading 'AI Summit 2026'. Date: March 15, Venue: Tokyo Big Sight. Include a QR code placeholder at the bottom."

Leverage Canva Pro

Some advanced features (premium templates, multiple brand kit management) require Canva Pro.

Summary

The Canva × Claude integration enables anyone to create professional designs through chat alone, regardless of design tool expertise. The brand kit integration is particularly powerful for maintaining consistent design quality across teams.

If you regularly create presentations or social media images, give this integration a try.

FAQ

Does it work with Canva's free plan?

Yes, basic features work with Canva's free plan. However, premium templates and advanced brand kit features require Canva Pro.

Which Claude plan supports this?

The connector feature is available on Claude.ai Pro plan and above. It's also available in Cowork.

Who owns the copyright for generated designs?

This follows Canva's terms of service. Designs created in Canva are available for commercial use (subject to Canva's asset licensing terms).

en.title: Gemini × Claude Guide — Maximize Productivity by Combining Two AIs
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: Compare Google Gemini and Claude, then learn practical multi-AI workflows that leverage each platform's strengths for maximum productivity.
en.content:
Introduction

In 2026, the debate over "which AI is best" is becoming irrelevant. Professionals have moved to multi-AI workflows that leverage different models for different tasks.

Over 80% of enterprises now use three or more AI models in tandem. The Claude and Gemini combination is particularly powerful, as their strengths are highly complementary.

This guide helps you understand each platform's advantages and build practical workflows that use both.

Claude vs Gemini: Strengths Comparison

Where Claude Excels

Claude is built for deep reasoning and precision.

  • Coding: 80.9% on SWE-bench Verified. Excels at complex refactoring and architecture design
  • Structured writing: Technical documentation, reports, and detailed analysis with logical flow
  • Claude Code / Cowork: Terminal and desktop AI tools for direct development and automation
  • Careful reasoning: Step-by-step approach to ambiguous problems for accurate results
  • MCP (Model Context Protocol): Standardized integration with external tools and services

Where Gemini Excels

Gemini excels at multimodal processing and Google ecosystem integration.

  • Massive context window: Up to 2 million tokens. Process large volumes of materials at once
  • Google integration: Seamless connection to Gmail, Drive, Calendar, and Sheets
  • Multimodal processing: Analyze text, images, video, and audio across modalities
  • Real-time search: Integrated with Google Search for up-to-date information
  • Google AI Studio: Free platform for API access and experimentation

Head-to-Head Comparison

| Task | Claude | Gemini | |------|--------|--------| | Coding & Development | ◎ | ○ | | Technical Documentation | ◎ | ○ | | Bulk Document Analysis | ○ | ◎ | | Google Service Integration | ○ | ◎ | | Image & Video Analysis | ○ | ◎ | | Structured Long-form Writing | ◎ | ○ | | API & SDK Development | ◎ | ○ | | Real-time Information Search | ○ | ◎ |

Multi-AI Workflows in Practice

Pattern 1: Research → Implementation

The most common workflow pattern.

Phase 1: Research with Gemini

Leverage Gemini's massive context window to process large volumes of source material.

Ask Gemini:
"Read these 10 PDFs and summarize the latest AI industry
trends by category"

Gemini analyzes PDFs, web pages, and spreadsheets across modalities to produce a summary.

Phase 2: Build with Claude

Pass Gemini's analysis to Claude for the actual output.

Ask Claude:
"Based on the following research, create a technical blog
post from outline to full text.
[Paste Gemini's analysis]"

Claude generates a well-structured, high-quality document.

Pattern 2: Google Workspace → Development

Phase 1: Analyze Google Workspace data with Gemini

Ask Gemini:
"Analyze the sales data in my Google Sheet and summarize
this month's trends with action items"

Gemini accesses Google Drive files directly for analysis.

Phase 2: Build automation with Claude Code

Ask Claude Code:
"Based on this analysis, create a script that automatically
aggregates daily sales data"

Claude Code generates the script and runs tests.

Pattern 3: Multimodal Analysis → Reporting

Phase 1: Analyze visuals with Gemini

Ask Gemini:
"Analyze these 10 product screenshots and list UI/UX
improvements"

Phase 2: Create reports with Claude

Ask Claude Cowork:
"Based on this UI analysis, create an improvement
proposal as a Word document"

For Developers: API-Level Integration

Gemini API + Claude API Pipeline

Build pipelines combining both APIs:

Integration via MCP

Claude's Model Context Protocol enables seamless integration of Gemini outputs into Claude workflows.

💡
**Cost optimization tip:** Use Gemini's free tier (Google AI Studio) for research and analysis, then focus Claude's paid plan on high-quality output generation. This allocation strategy maximizes value from both platforms.

Real-World Scenarios

Scenario 1: Blog Writing

  1. Gemini: Research latest trends via Google Search integration
  2. Claude: Create outline and write the article based on research
  3. Gemini: Fact-check the finished article and supplement with latest data

Scenario 2: App Development

  1. Gemini: Analyze competitor app screenshots and generate feature comparison
  2. Claude Code: Design and implement the app based on analysis
  3. Gemini: Analyze Google Play / App Store reviews for feedback collection

Scenario 3: Data Analysis Reports

  1. Gemini: Directly analyze Google Sheets data
  2. Claude Cowork: Generate Excel report with charts from analysis results
  3. Gemini: Convert report content into Google Slides presentation

When to Use Which

Use Claude When

  • Writing, reviewing, or refactoring code
  • Creating technical documentation or API references
  • Complex logical reasoning is required
  • File operations and desktop automation (Cowork)
  • External service integration via MCP

Use Gemini When

  • Analyzing Google Workspace data
  • Bulk processing PDFs and documents
  • Analyzing images and videos
  • Research requiring real-time information
  • Tasks involving Google service integration

Either Works Well For

  • General Q&A
  • Short text generation
  • Brainstorming ideas
  • Translation

Summary

Claude and Gemini aren't competitors — they're complementary AI tools. Understanding each platform's strengths and applying them appropriately unlocks productivity levels impossible with any single AI.

The key to AI productivity in 2026 isn't choosing "the best AI." It's effectively combining multiple AIs. Start with simple tasks and experiment with using Claude and Gemini together.

FAQ

Is Claude or Gemini better?

Neither is universally better. Claude excels at coding and technical writing, while Gemini is stronger at bulk document processing and Google service integration. Purpose-driven selection is most effective.

Can I use both for free?

Yes. Both Claude and Gemini offer free plans. However, full features require paid plans for each.

How much does API integration cost?

Gemini's Google AI Studio has a generous free tier, and Claude API uses pay-as-you-go pricing starting from small amounts. You can build sophisticated pipelines for roughly $20-50/month.

en.title: Aqua Voice × Claude Guide — Accelerate AI Interaction with Voice Input
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: Learn how to use Aqua Voice to speed up your interaction with Claude (Cowork / Claude Code). From setup to practical techniques, plus a comparison with Claude's built-in voice modes.
en.content:
What Is Aqua Voice?

Aqua Voice is an AI-powered voice dictation tool for Mac and Windows, developed by a Y Combinator (W24) startup. It features a proprietary speech recognition model called "Avalon" that sets it apart from generic dictation tools.

The key differentiator is its ability to read screen context to improve recognition accuracy. For programming terms and technical jargon, Aqua Voice achieves approximately 17x better accuracy than Siri or Google Voice Typing, making it especially popular among developers and technical writers.

Why It Works Great with Claude

Whether you're using Cowork or Claude Code, interacting with Claude is fundamentally text-based. The more complex your instructions, the more typing you need to do.

Aqua Voice inserts text directly into whatever text field has focus — Cowork's chat input, Claude Code's terminal prompt, or any browser input field. It works anywhere your cursor is.

Since speaking averages around 150 words per minute (roughly 3-4x faster than typing), the time savings become significant when crafting detailed prompts.

Setup

1. Installation

Download and install from the official website (aquavoice.com). It supports Mac (Apple Silicon and Intel) and Windows 10/11.

2. Basic Usage

After installation, Aqua Voice runs in the menu bar (Mac) or system tray (Windows). Press your configured shortcut key to start dictation.

The shortcut is fully customizable. Startup time is under 50ms, and text insertion takes about 1 second (as fast as 450ms).

3. Custom Dictionary

Register technical terms and proper nouns in the custom dictionary for better recognition. For example:

  • Claude Code — AI coding tool name
  • Cowork — Claude desktop feature
  • MCP — Model Context Protocol abbreviation
  • SKILL.md — Skill definition file name

Practical Usage

With Cowork

Place your cursor in Cowork's chat input, activate Aqua Voice, and simply speak your instructions.

For example, you can say something like "Read all the PDFs in this folder and create an Excel file summarizing their contents" — and it types it out for you. Aqua Voice automatically adds punctuation and formatting, so the output reads naturally.

With Claude Code

Claude Code runs in the terminal, and Aqua Voice works directly with terminal input fields.

Technical terms, commands, and file paths are recognized with high accuracy thanks to screen context reading. Programming terms like useEffect, kubectl, and PyTorch achieve 97% recognition accuracy.

Paste Shortcut

A dedicated shortcut lets you re-paste the last transcribed text:

  • Mac: Cmd + Ctrl + V
  • Windows: Ctrl + Shift + →

This is useful when you want to send the same instruction to a different window.

Comparison with Claude's Built-in Voice Modes

Claude already offers several voice input options.

Claude.ai Voice Mode

Claude.ai (web and mobile) includes a built-in conversational voice mode for real-time voice conversations, but it's limited to the web interface.

Claude Code /voice Command

Claude Code has a built-in /voice command — hold Space, speak, and release to transcribe. It's convenient but relies on generic speech recognition.

Where Aqua Voice Excels

Aqua Voice differentiates itself in several ways:

  1. Works everywhere: Cowork, Claude Code, browsers, email, Slack — anywhere there's a text cursor
  2. Technical term recognition: Reads screen context to accurately transcribe programming terminology
  3. 49 languages: Natural switching between languages including Japanese and English
  4. Low latency: Under 50ms startup, approximately 1 second text insertion

That said, Claude Code's /voice requires no installation and works out of the box, making it perfectly adequate for quick, simple prompts. Aqua Voice shines when you need to dictate longer prompts or require precise technical terminology.

Pricing

Aqua Voice offers the following plans:

  • Free: Basic features with limitations
  • Pro: $8/month ($96/year) — Full features
  • Team: $12/month — Centralized billing, org-wide privacy settings

Start with the Free plan to get a feel for it, then consider upgrading to Pro if it becomes part of your daily workflow.

Summary

Aqua Voice is a powerful complement to Claude that can significantly boost your productivity, especially when:

  • You frequently write long, detailed prompts
  • You need accurate transcription of programming terms
  • You want hands-free Claude interaction
  • You work across multiple languages

Think at the speed of thought, not at the speed of typing — that's what Aqua Voice and Claude together make possible.

FAQ

Is Aqua Voice free to use?

Yes, there's a Free plan with basic features. For full functionality, you can upgrade to the Pro plan at $8/month.

How does it differ from Claude Code's /voice?

Claude Code's /voice is a convenient built-in option, but Aqua Voice uses a proprietary model that reads screen context for dramatically better technical term recognition, and it works across all applications.

Does it support languages other than English?

Aqua Voice supports 49 languages with high accuracy. You can also register specialized terms in the custom dictionary to further improve recognition.

en.title: MCP × Claude Practical Guide — Best Servers and Integration Scenarios
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: A practical deep-dive into MCP (Model Context Protocol) with Claude. Covers top MCP servers like GitHub, Figma, and Sentry, with real-world integration scenarios to supercharge your development workflow.
en.content:
How MCP Transforms the Claude Experience

MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open standard protocol announced by Anthropic in November 2024. Designed as a unified interface connecting AI models to external tools and data sources, it has grown in just one year to an ecosystem with over 97 million monthly SDK downloads and 10,000+ active servers.

Claude Code, Claude Desktop, and many other clients including Cursor and VS Code natively support MCP—simply adding an MCP server infinitely extends Claude's capabilities.

ℹ️
**Core MCP Concepts:** - **Tools** — Actions Claude can execute (API calls, DB queries, etc.) - **Resources** — Data sources Claude can read (files, DB records, etc.) - **Prompts** — Pre-defined templates and workflows

Top 7 Recommended MCP Servers

1. GitHub MCP Server

GitHub's official MCP server is the first server adopted by over 90% of Claude Code users. It provides Claude access to nearly all GitHub features: repository operations, PR creation and review, issue management, code search, and CI/CD status monitoring.

Use Case: Instructions like "Check the review comments on this PR and fix them" or "Create a branch based on Issue #42 and write the code" work naturally.

2. Filesystem MCP Server

Provides access to the local filesystem. It includes explicit permission gates for safety, allowing Claude to directly read, write, search, and organize files.

Use Case: Hand your entire project folder to Claude with requests like "Organize the file structure in this directory" or "Find and clean up old log files."

3. Figma MCP Server (Dev Mode)

Figma's official Dev Mode MCP server lets Claude generate code directly from design data. Instead of working from screenshots, it understands the actual design structure—layers, components, Auto Layout, and design tokens—enabling high-accuracy code conversion.

Use Case: "Convert this Figma frame to a React component" or "Update styles to match the design tokens."

4. Sentry MCP Server

This server integrates with real-time error tracking. Claude can retrieve full error context—stack traces, user impact, frequency—from Sentry without opening the UI and suggest fixes.

Use Case: "Show me today's error list" or "Identify the cause of this TypeError and write a fix."

5. Brave Search MCP Server

Adds web search capabilities to Claude. When you need to check the latest documentation or library information during development, you can get search results directly within the conversation.

6. PostgreSQL / SQLite MCP Server

Enables direct database querying. Useful for verifying data during development, inserting test data, and analyzing schemas through Claude.

7. Cloudflare MCP Server

Manage Cloudflare resources—Workers, KV, D1, R2—through Claude. Convenient for checking deployment status and modifying worker configurations.

Remote MCP Servers and Authentication

MCP initially centered on local execution (stdio), but now supports remote servers as well.

Streamable HTTP Transport

The current MCP standard transport is Streamable HTTP. The legacy SSE (Server-Sent Events) method is being gradually deprecated.

OAuth 2.1 Authentication

Remote MCP servers support authentication via the OAuth 2.1 protocol. Claude's OAuth callback URL is https://claude.ai/api/mcp/auth_callback, with support for token expiry and refresh.

⚠️
**Security Best Practices:** - Treat each MCP server as a microservice with its own blast radius - Require TLS and client-server authentication - Scope API keys to minimum privileges; start high-impact servers in read-only mode - Manage credentials like `GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` via environment variables

Practical Scenarios: Automating Development with MCP

Scenario 1: Issue → Code → PR Full Automation

Combining GitHub MCP + Filesystem MCP lets Claude read issue requirements, create a branch, write code, and open a PR.

You: Read the requirements from Issue #15 and implement them, then create a PR
Claude: Let me check Issue #15... It's about "Dark mode support."
        I'll create a feature/dark-mode branch and start implementing.
        ...(coding)...
        PR #23 has been created. Please review.

Scenario 2: Error Detection → Root Cause → Fix

By chaining Sentry MCP + GitHub MCP, you can handle everything from production error detection to creating a fix PR in one flow.

Scenario 3: Design → Code → Deploy

Linking Figma MCP → Filesystem MCP → GitHub MCP → Cloudflare MCP lets Claude assist the entire pipeline from design to deployment.

Configuring MCP Servers

In Claude Code

In Claude Desktop

Add MCP server configurations to claude_desktop_config.json:

Summary

MCP has rapidly gained adoption as the standard protocol for connecting Claude to external tools. By combining key servers like GitHub, Figma, and Sentry, Claude can assist your entire development workflow—from issue management and design conversion to error handling and deployment.

Start with the GitHub MCP server and add more servers as your project needs grow. That's the most practical approach.

en.title: Rork × Claude App Development — Refine AI-Generated Apps with Claude Code
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: Practical guide to refining Rork and Rork Max generated mobile apps with Claude Code. Covers Rork Max native Swift, 2-click App Store publishing, and more.
en.content:
Introduction

Mobile app development is complex and time-consuming. However, the evolution of AI is dramatically changing the landscape. The combination of Rork and Claude Code can drastically shorten the path from idea to production-quality app.

This guide walks you through a complete workflow: rapidly generating a prototype with Rork, then refining it into a polished, feature-complete application using Claude Code.

What is Rork?

Rork is an AI-powered app builder that generates real, production-capable mobile applications (React Native / Expo) from natural language prompts. In as little as a few minutes, you can go from a text description to a working app with complete UI, navigation, and styling.

ℹ️
**Key Rork Features:** - Generate complete React Native projects from text prompts in minutes - Integrated with Expo for real-time device previews - Exportable source code for continued development locally - Seamless GitHub synchronization for version control - Built-in support for App Store and Google Play deployment

Rork excels at generating the foundational structure that would normally take a development team weeks. However, generated apps are best viewed as "starters"—scaffolds that need refinement to reach production quality. This is where Claude Code becomes invaluable.

Rork Max — The Native Swift Era

In February 2026, Rork launched Rork Max, a completely new product separate from the React Native-based Rork.

What Makes Rork Max Different

Rork Max is powered by Claude Code and Opus and generates native Swift apps. The biggest advantage is deep access to Apple platform capabilities that React Native simply cannot reach.

ℹ️
**Rork Max Supported Platforms:** - iPhone / iPad (iOS / iPadOS) - Apple Watch (watchOS) - Apple TV (tvOS) - Vision Pro (visionOS) - iMessage Apps

Native vs React Native

Traditional Rork builds cross-platform apps (iOS + Android) with React Native / Expo. Rork Max focuses exclusively on the Apple ecosystem, unlocking full native capabilities:

  • ARKit / LiDAR Scanning — Augmented reality experiences, 3D object placement
  • Metal Graphics — 3D games, advanced visuals
  • Home Screen Widgets / Dynamic Island / Live Activities
  • Siri Intents — Voice command integration
  • HealthKit / HomeKit / NFC
  • Core ML — On-device machine learning
  • App Clips — Lightweight app experiences without installation

2-Click App Store Publishing

The most notable Rork Max feature is publishing to the App Store without Xcode and without a Mac.

  1. Build your app in the browser
  2. Compile natively on Rork's cloud Mac fleet
  3. Preview via streaming simulator
  4. Install on your device via QR code
  5. Submit to the App Store in 2 clicks

You'll still need an Apple Developer Program membership ($99/year), but Xcode downloads and Mac ownership are not required.

Rork Max Pricing

  • Free: Approximately 5 prompts per week (trial)
  • Max Plan: $200/month — Unlimited native Swift builds, all platforms

Rork Max hit $1.5M ARR within just 3 days of launch, reflecting enormous interest from the developer community.

Rork Max × Claude Code Workflow

Native Swift projects generated by Rork Max can also be exported and refined with Claude Code. For Swift / SwiftUI codebases, Claude Code can help with:

  • Refactoring to Swift best practices
  • Applying MVVM architecture
  • Introducing Swift Concurrency (async/await)
  • Generating XCTest test suites
  • Adding data persistence with Core Data or SwiftData
💡
**Which to choose:** If you need cross-platform (iOS + Android), use traditional Rork. If you want to build for the Apple ecosystem with native features like ARKit, widgets, and Vision Pro, choose Rork Max.

Generating Apps with Rork

Basic Workflow

Using Rork to generate an app is straightforward:

  1. Describe Your App — Write a natural language description: "Task management app with user authentication and dark mode support"
  2. AI Generates Code — Rork's AI parses your description and auto-generates React Native components, navigation, and styling
  3. Real-Time Preview — Use Expo Go to see your app on a device instantly
  4. Export and Iterate — Download the complete React Native project and continue development

Prompt Engineering Tips

The quality of generated apps depends heavily on prompt clarity and specificity:

Good Example:

Create a weather app with:
- A main screen showing current temperature and conditions
- Search functionality to find other cities
- A 5-day forecast section
- Temperature conversion (Celsius/Fahrenheit)
- Dark mode support with a toggle button in the header

Poor Example:

Make a weather app

Include specific features, UI layout preferences, and design requirements for better results.

Generated Project Structure

Rork creates a standard React Native / Expo project with this structure:

my-app/
├── app/
│   ├── (tabs)/
│   │   ├── index.tsx
│   │   ├── explore.tsx
│   │   └── profile.tsx
│   ├── _layout.tsx
│   └── +html.tsx
├── components/
│   ├── ThemedText.tsx
│   ├── ThemedView.tsx
│   └── Navigation.tsx
├── constants/
│   ├── Colors.ts
│   └── Layout.ts
├── hooks/
│   └── useColorScheme.ts
├── app.json
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── .gitignore

This structure leverages Expo Router, using file-based routing conventions.

Limitations of Generated Apps

While Rork excels at rapid prototyping, generated apps have inherent limitations:

Generic Design

Generated apps use a basic design system (Colors.ts, layout constants, etc.). Production apps require brand-aligned custom styling.

Incomplete Business Logic

AI can generate UI skeletons and mock data, but real API integration, authentication, and database operations require developer implementation.

No Tests

Generated code includes no test coverage. Unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests must be added separately.

Performance Not Optimized

Generated code isn't optimized. Component memoization, image optimization, bundle size reduction, and other performance improvements are deferred.

⚠️
Avoid deploying generated apps directly to production. Always use Claude Code for review and enhancement first.

Refining with Claude Code

Step 1: Prepare Your Project

Set up the generated Rork project for Claude Code development:

  1. Download from Rork — Export your React Native project
  2. Initialize Git — Run git init to start version control
  3. Create CLAUDE.md — Document project context, architecture, and development guidelines

Example CLAUDE.md:

Step 2: Load Project in Claude Code

Claude Code loads your project and starts an interactive development session.

Step 3: Request Improvements

Provide Claude Code with specific improvement requests.

UI/UX Enhancements

Example 1: Modern Login Screen

Claude Code automatically:

  • Generates or updates stylesheet files
  • Adds animation libraries (react-native-reanimated, etc.)
  • Refactors components with new styles
  • Presents changes for review

Example 2: Complete Dark Mode Implementation

Adding Business Logic

API Integration

Rork-generated apps typically include mock data. Use Claude Code to integrate real APIs:

User Authentication

Data Persistence

Adding Tests

Jest Test Suite Generation

Rork-generated components lack tests. Add comprehensive coverage with Claude Code:

Claude Code generates:

  • __tests__/components — Component tests
  • __tests__/services — API service tests
  • jest.config.js — Jest configuration
  • __mocks__ — Mocks for APIs and libraries

Running Tests

Performance Optimization

Rendering Optimization

Image Optimization

Bundle Size Reduction

Advanced Integration with Claude Code MCP

Claude Code's Model Context Protocol (MCP) enables deeper automation of Rork project operations.

ℹ️
MCP is a standard protocol allowing Claude Code to interact with external tools and services. When Rork MCP servers become available, you'll be able to:
  • Manage projects directly through the Rork API
  • Trigger previews from within Claude Code
  • Seamlessly combine Rork's generation with Claude Code's refinement

Complete Practical Workflow

Let's walk through a real-world project lifecycle.

Phase 1: Rork Prototype Generation (30 minutes)

Prompt: "Create a task management app with:
- Task list showing incomplete tasks
- Add new task button
- Mark task as complete/incomplete
- Delete task functionality
- Task categories
- Dark mode support"

Result: Basic UI skeleton, navigation, styling

Phase 2: Code Review with Claude Code (30 minutes)

Claude Code generates a comprehensive review report.

Phase 3: Feature Completion (2-3 hours)

Phase 4: Testing and QA (2-3 hours)

Tests are completed and bugs are fixed.

Phase 5: Deployment Preparation (1 hour)

Phase 6: App Store and Google Play Publication

Rork provides direct integration support for app store deployment.

ℹ️
This entire workflow can complete a project in **1-2 days** that normally takes a week.

Best Practices

1. Incremental Improvements

Make changes in stages rather than attempting everything at once:

2. Keep CLAUDE.md Updated

As your project evolves, update CLAUDE.md to reflect the current state. This ensures Claude Code always has the latest context.

3. Manage Git Commits

Create commits for each phase to track changes:

4. Give Specific Instructions

Provide concrete requirements rather than vague directives:

Vague:

"Improve the app"

Specific:

"Performance optimization:
1. Memoize the TaskList component
2. Use FlatList instead of ScrollView for better performance
3. Add image caching
4. Reduce bundle size by removing unused dependencies"

5. Prioritize Code Review

Even auto-generated code requires thorough review before production deployment.

Troubleshooting

Build Errors

Performance Issues

Test Failures

Summary

The Rork and Claude Code combination significantly improves mobile app development speed and quality:

  1. Rork generates prototypes rapidly
  2. Claude Code refines code, adds business logic, and handles edge cases
  3. Automated tests ensure coverage and quality
  4. Incremental improvements drive toward production quality
  5. Deploy with confidence

This workflow dramatically reduces development time while maintaining quality. Leverage Rork's speed and Claude Code's refinement to build your next app efficiently.

Related Resources

  • Rork Official Site
  • Rork FAQ
  • Claude Code Documentation
  • Expo Router Documentation
  • React Native Testing Library

en.title: Claude Code Voice Mode Complete Guide — Hands-Free Coding from Your Terminal
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: Learn how to use Claude Code's Voice Mode for hands-free coding. Complete guide covering the /voice command, Push-to-Talk, supported languages, configuration, and third-party STT tool integration.
en.content:
import { Callout } from '@/components/ui/callout';

What Is Claude Code Voice Mode?

On March 3, 2026, Anthropic officially began rolling out native Voice Mode for Claude Code. This feature lets developers issue spoken commands directly from the terminal, enabling a hands-free coding workflow.

Instead of typing out every instruction, you can now speak naturally — saying things like "refactor the authentication middleware" or "explain this failing test" — while Claude Code transcribes and processes your commands.

ℹ️
Voice Mode is currently in a gradual rollout as of March 2026. It's available to approximately 5% of users initially, with broader access expected over the coming weeks. Available on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans at no additional cost.

How to Enable Voice Mode

The /voice Command

Simply type the following slash command during any Claude Code session to toggle voice mode on:

Once enabled, you'll see an indicator in your terminal prompt confirming that voice input is available. To disable it, type /voice again.

Push-to-Talk Mechanism

Voice Mode uses a Push-to-Talk approach — there's no always-listening mode, so you don't have to worry about background noise triggering unintended input.

Here's how it works:

  1. Hold the spacebar to start recording
  2. Speak naturally about what you want Claude to do
  3. Release the spacebar — your speech is transcribed (STT) and inserted into the prompt
[Hold spacebar] → Speak → [Release spacebar] → Text appears in input field
💡
Voice input is inserted as text at your cursor position. This means you can freely mix typed and spoken input within a single prompt. For example, type the beginning of a command and use voice to complete the rest.

Customizing the Activation Key

The default activation key is the spacebar, but you can customize it in keybindings.json. Using a modifier+letter combination like meta+k eliminates any interference with normal typing.

Voice Input Only (Text Output)

The current Voice Mode is input-only. You speak your instructions, and Claude responds with text. There is no text-to-speech (TTS) output at this time.

This is an intentional design choice — developers need to read code and terminal output, making text responses more practical for coding workflows.

Supported Languages

The March 2026 update expanded Voice Mode STT (speech recognition) to a total of 20 languages.

In addition to the original 10 languages, the following 10 were newly added:

  • Russian
  • Polish
  • Turkish
  • Dutch
  • Ukrainian
  • Greek
  • Czech
  • Danish
  • Swedish
  • Norwegian
⚠️
For languages not yet natively supported (such as Japanese), third-party STT tools like Aqua Voice or VoiceMode MCP can be used as alternatives. See the "Third-Party STT Tools" section below.

Why Voice Mode Transforms Development Workflows

1. Maintaining Flow State

Typing long prompts breaks your train of thought. With voice input, you can articulate ideas as they come to mind, staying in "vibe coding" mode where you describe what you want conversationally while the AI handles the details.

2. Communicating Complex Instructions

Instructions like "Replace the state management in this component with useReducer, add type definitions for the actions, and write tests too" are faster and more natural to speak than to type.

3. Multitasking

You can review code on screen while simultaneously giving Claude voice instructions for modifications — true parallel workflows become possible.

4. Accessibility

For developers with hand or arm strain, voice input serves as a critical accessibility tool. It can significantly reduce the risk of RSI (Repetitive Strain Injury).

Practical Usage Examples

Code Review

[Hold spacebar]
"Review the diff for this PR and check for security issues.
Focus specifically on SQL injection and XSS vulnerabilities."
[Release spacebar]

Debugging

[Hold spacebar]
"Investigate why this test is failing.
Read the error message, identify the root cause, and suggest a fix."
[Release spacebar]

Refactoring

[Hold spacebar]
"Refactor this auth middleware to follow Express middleware patterns
and add proper error handling."
[Release spacebar]

Project Architecture

[Hold spacebar]
"Review the directory structure of this project and suggest improvements
based on Next.js App Router best practices."
[Release spacebar]

Third-Party STT Tool Integration

In addition to native Voice Mode, third-party speech-to-text tools offer alternative approaches — particularly useful for languages not yet natively supported.

Aqua Voice

Aqua Voice is an AI-powered dictation tool that reads screen context to improve recognition accuracy. It has exceptionally high accuracy for programming terminology and works directly in the Claude Code terminal.

For details, see our Aqua Voice × Claude Guide.

VoiceMode MCP Server

VoiceMode MCP integrates with Claude Code as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. It uses OpenAI Whisper for STT and OpenAI TTS for speech output.

The key advantage of VoiceMode MCP is that it enables voice output (TTS) from Claude, not just voice input.

Claude STT

Claude STT is an open-source tool that adds streaming speech-to-text input to Claude Code.

Native Voice Mode vs Third-Party Tools Comparison

| Feature | Native Voice Mode | Aqua Voice | VoiceMode MCP | Claude STT | |---------|-------------------|------------|---------------|------------| | Setup | /voice only | App install | MCP config | npm install | | Activation | Spacebar | OS hotkey | Via MCP | Ctrl+Shift+Space | | Japanese Support | Unconfirmed | Yes | Via Whisper | Via Whisper | | Voice Output | No | No | Yes (TTS) | No | | Extra Cost | None | Monthly plan | OpenAI API fees | OpenAI API fees | | Offline | Unknown | No | No | Local Whisper possible |

Requirements and Limitations

Supported Plans

Voice Mode is available on the following plans at no additional cost:

  • Pro Plan
  • Max Plan
  • Team Plan
  • Enterprise Plan

Free plan users do not currently have access.

Current Limitations

  • Gradual rollout: Available to approximately 5% of users as of March 2026, expanding over coming weeks
  • Terminal only: VS Code extension and desktop app support is unconfirmed
  • Input only: No text-to-speech (TTS) responses
  • Language coverage: Not all languages are natively supported yet

Troubleshooting

Voice Mode Not Appearing

The rollout may not have reached your account yet. Check the following:

  1. Update Claude Code to the latest version
  2. Confirm you're on a paid plan (Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise)
  3. Wait a few days and check again

Speech Not Recognized Correctly

  • Use voice mode in a quiet environment
  • Verify your microphone is properly configured
  • Press the spacebar before you start speaking, and release after you finish

Spacebar Interference

If the spacebar triggers Voice Mode during normal typing, change the activation key:

What's Next for Voice Mode

Claude Code Voice Mode is still in its early stages, but several enhancements are expected:

  • Expanded language support: Native support for Asian languages including Japanese
  • Voice output (TTS): Audio responses from Claude
  • IDE integration: Voice Mode support in VS Code and JetBrains IDEs
  • Enhanced context awareness: More accurate speech recognition that understands code context
  • Hands-free mode: Always-listening mode in addition to Push-to-Talk

FAQ

Q: Can I use Voice Mode on the free plan?

A: No, Voice Mode is currently available only on paid plans (Pro, Max, Team, Enterprise). There is no additional charge for these plan holders.

Q: Can I still type normally with Voice Mode enabled?

A: Yes. Voice Mode only activates when you hold the spacebar, so regular typing works as expected. If spacebar interference is a concern, change the activation key to a modifier combination.

Q: Does Voice Mode work offline?

A: Claude Code is a cloud-based service that requires an internet connection. Speech recognition processing is also presumed to be server-side.

Q: Is there any mode other than Push-to-Talk?

A: Currently, Push-to-Talk is the only input method. There is no always-listening (hands-free) mode. This design prevents background noise from triggering unintended input.

Q: Can I combine Voice Mode with other Claude Code features?

A: Yes. Voice Mode works alongside all existing Claude Code features including MCP servers, agent teams, hooks, and plugins. It's simply an alternative input method — everything else functions exactly as before.

en.title: Figma × Claude Code Integration Guide — Bridging Design and Code with MCP
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: Learn how to connect Figma and Claude Code via MCP server for bidirectional design-to-code workflows, from setup to practical techniques.
en.content:
What Is Figma × Claude Code Integration?

In February 2026, Figma officially announced a bidirectional integration with Claude Code. Using Figma's MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, Claude Code can read design data from Figma to generate code, and conversely push UIs built in Claude Code back to Figma as editable layers.

This integration dramatically streamlines the designer-developer handoff. Instead of visually inspecting designs and manually writing code, AI automatically understands the design structure — layers, components, Auto Layout, and design tokens — and translates it into production-ready code.

ℹ️
**Key capabilities of Figma MCP integration:** - Retrieve design context from Figma to generate code (Design to Code) - Push UIs built in Claude Code back to Figma (Code to Canvas) - AI-generated FigJam diagrams - Design system / component understanding for consistent code output - Component mapping via Code Connect

Prerequisites

To get started with Figma × Claude Code integration, you'll need:

  • Claude Code installed and configured (see Setup Guide)
  • Figma account (basic features available on free plans)
  • Figma desktop app (required for the desktop MCP server option)
⚠️
**Plan considerations:** The free plan allows you to try Code to Canvas via the remote MCP server, but with a limit of 6 tool calls per month. For practical usage, a Professional plan or above with a Dev/Full seat is recommended.

Setup

There are two ways to connect to the Figma MCP server. Choose based on your needs.

Option 1: Remote MCP Server (Recommended)

Connect to Figma's cloud-hosted MCP server. No desktop app required — the easiest way to get started.

This opens Figma's authentication page in your browser. Log in and grant access to complete the setup.

Option 2: Desktop MCP Server

Use the local server running from the Figma desktop app. This offers faster response times and partial offline functionality.

Step 1: Enable MCP in Figma Desktop

  1. Open the Figma desktop app
  2. Go to Figma → Settings
  3. Toggle Enable desktop MCP server on

Step 2: Register the local server in Claude Code

Step 3: Verify the connection

If figma or figma-desktop appears in the list, you're connected.

Setup via Figma Plugin

Figma also provides a dedicated Claude Code plugin. Installing it sets up the MCP server configuration along with agent skills for common workflows.

Design to Code

Basic Usage

To convert Figma designs into code with Claude Code, select a frame or layer in Figma, copy its link, and pass it to Claude Code.

Step 1: Get the link from Figma

  1. Select the frame or component you want to code
  2. Right-click → Copy link to selection (or Ctrl/Cmd + L)

Step 2: Ask Claude Code

Please implement this Figma design as a React component.
Use Tailwind CSS and make it responsive.

https://www.figma.com/design/xxxxx/MyProject?node-id=123-456

Claude Code reads the design data via MCP — layer structure, colors, fonts, spacing, Auto Layout settings — and generates code that faithfully matches the design.

Working with Design Systems

When connected to a Figma design system (component library), the AI understands existing components and produces consistent output.

Review all Button component variants (Primary, Secondary, Ghost)
from the project's design system, and create React components
that match the existing codebase.

When Code Connect is configured, Claude Code understands the mapping between design components and actual code components, reusing existing components in the generated code.

Practical Tip: Section-by-Section Implementation

Instead of implementing an entire page at once, working section by section yields better accuracy.

First, implement the header section (node-id=10-20) from this
Figma page. Navigation links can use placeholder data for now.

https://www.figma.com/design/xxxxx/MyProject?node-id=10-20

Code to Canvas

Live UI Capture

You can push UIs built in Claude Code back to Figma — not as screenshots, but as editable Figma layers.

Please push the current UI back to Figma.
The file is https://www.figma.com/design/xxxxx/MyProject

Claude Code captures the rendered UI and reconstructs it as Figma shapes, text, and image layers. Designers can then directly edit and refine the output in Figma.

Iterative Workflow

The real power of Code to Canvas lies in iterative workflows:

  1. Designer creates mockups in Figma
  2. Claude Code reads the design and implements the code
  3. Developer adjusts and improves the code
  4. Claude Code pushes the updated UI back to Figma
  5. Designer provides feedback and makes corrections in Figma
  6. Repeat steps 2–5

This bidirectional flow minimizes the gap between design intent and actual implementation.

FigJam × Claude

Figma MCP also supports FigJam integration. Pass text, PDFs, or images to Claude, and it can auto-generate structured diagrams on FigJam.

Create a system architecture diagram on FigJam
based on these project requirements.

[Requirements text or PDF]

Common use cases include user flow visualization, system architecture diagrams, and brainstorming session organization.

Community MCP Servers

Beyond Figma's official MCP server, the community has developed open-source alternatives.

Claude Talk to Figma

A community-built alternative that provides some functionality without requiring a Dev Mode license. It works with Claude Desktop, Cursor, and other AI tools.

⚠️
Community tools are unofficial and may break with Figma updates. For production workflows, the official MCP server is recommended.

Troubleshooting

MCP Connection Fails

Desktop MCP Server Not Responding

  1. Confirm the Figma desktop app is running
  2. Verify that the MCP server is enabled in settings
  3. Check if http://127.0.0.1:3845/sse responds in your browser

Low Code Generation Accuracy

  • Ensure your Figma design data is well-organized (proper layer naming, appropriate Auto Layout usage)
  • Give instructions at the component level rather than full frames
  • Explicitly specify the target framework (React, Vue, Svelte, etc.)

Summary

The Figma × Claude Code MCP integration fundamentally transforms the design-development workflow. Design to Code accurately translates design intent into working code, while Code to Canvas feeds implementation results back to designers — creating a truly bidirectional flow.

Start with the remote MCP server connection, and as you get comfortable with the workflow, consider adopting the desktop MCP server and design system integration for a more powerful setup.

Related Articles

  • Claude Code Setup Guide
  • MCP Servers Guide
  • Claude Code IDE Integration Guide
  • Claude Code Practical Workflows
  • Cursor × Claude Editor Integration Guide

en.title: API Rate Limits & Best Practices — Efficient Token Management and Scaling Strategies
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: Understand how Claude API rate limits work and learn best practices for optimizing token usage, improving request efficiency, and building scaling strategies for production environments.
en.content:
API Rate Limits & Best Practices

Running Claude API reliably in production requires a solid understanding of how rate limits work and how to design efficient request patterns. This guide covers rate limit structures, token optimization techniques, and scaling strategies.

How Rate Limits Work

Rate limits ensure API stability and fair resource distribution across all users.

Types of Limits

Claude API applies rate limits on three axes:

| Limit Type | Description | Unit | |---|---|---| | RPM (Requests Per Minute) | Maximum requests per minute | Request count | | TPM (Tokens Per Minute) | Maximum input + output tokens per minute | Token count | | TPD (Tokens Per Day) | Maximum input + output tokens per day | Token count |

Plan-Based Limits

Rate limits vary by plan and model. Check Anthropic's documentation for the latest values. Higher-tier plans generally receive higher limits.

Using Response Headers

API responses include headers showing your current rate limit status. Use these to control request flow:

RateLimit-Limit-Requests: 50
RateLimit-Limit-Tokens: 90000
RateLimit-Remaining-Requests: 35
RateLimit-Remaining-Tokens: 72000
RateLimit-Reset-Requests: 2026-03-09T12:00:30Z
RateLimit-Reset-Tokens: 2026-03-09T12:00:30Z
Retry-After: 15

Optimizing Token Usage

Optimizing token consumption improves both cost efficiency and rate limit headroom.

1. Prompt Optimization

Write concise, effective prompts to reduce input tokens:

2. Setting max_tokens Appropriately

Set max_tokens to the minimum needed for your use case:

3. Efficient System Prompts

System prompts are sent with every request, so brevity matters:

4. Pre-Checking Token Counts

Estimate token counts before sending requests to avoid rate limit overruns:

Request Efficiency Techniques

1. Request Queuing

Queue requests with flow control to prevent hitting rate limits:

2. Using Batch Processing

For high-volume workloads, the Message Batches API helps avoid rate limit pressure:

Key benefits of batch processing:

  • Rate limits are managed separately from real-time requests
  • 50% cost discount
  • Efficient handling of large request volumes
  • Results delivered within 24 hours

3. Leveraging Prompt Caching

Use caching for repeated prompts to reduce token reprocessing:

Cache hits reduce input token costs by 90%.

Monitoring and Visibility

Usage Tracking

Scaling Strategies

Gradual Scale-Up

For production deployments, increase traffic gradually:

  1. Development: Default rate limits are sufficient
  2. Testing: Consider plan upgrades if load tests hit limits
  3. Early production: Monitor real traffic and adjust
  4. Scale phase: Contact Anthropic for custom rate limits

Requesting Higher Limits

If default limits are insufficient, request increases through the Anthropic Console. Prepare the following information:

  • Current usage patterns (actual RPM, TPM values)
  • Required limits with justification
  • Use case description
  • Expected traffic growth rate

Summary

Rate limits are essential for API stability. Keep these principles in mind for efficient API operations:

  • Monitor usage in real-time using response headers
  • Optimize token consumption to improve both cost and rate limit headroom
  • Actively leverage batch processing and prompt caching
  • Follow a gradual scaling strategy for stable growth
  • Request rate limit increases when needed

en.title: Best Books to Learn AI & LLMs — 2026 Edition
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: Curated list of the best books for learning about Claude, generative AI, prompt engineering, and LLM development. Organized by skill level for 2026.
en.content:
Introduction — Why Learn from Books?

The rapid evolution of generative AI technology, from ChatGPT to Claude, has fundamentally transformed how we work and learn. Yet to truly master these powerful tools, going beyond casual exploration is essential. Understanding the theory and technology behind large language models enables more creative, strategic, and effective applications in both personal and professional contexts.

Reading books offers distinct advantages over blog posts and tutorials. They provide systematic, in-depth knowledge organized by experienced practitioners and researchers. Rather than piecing together fragmented information from various online sources, a well-crafted book delivers comprehensive understanding, tested approaches, and best practices that have been carefully refined. This guide curates the most valuable books for learning AI and LLMs, organized by skill level—from complete beginners to experienced developers seeking to build production-grade systems.

AI & Generative AI Fundamentals (Beginner Level)

For those seeking to understand how generative AI works and how to apply it effectively in real-world scenarios, these foundational books provide both conceptual clarity and practical skills.

Practical Guides to Generative AI in Business

As generative AI reshapes industries, professionals increasingly need to understand how to integrate these tools into existing workflows. The fundamental question isn't just "what is AI?" but rather "how do I use this in my job?" Books in this category answer that question with concrete, actionable advice.

These practical guides explain the capabilities and limitations of AI systems through real business scenarios. They demonstrate how AI can enhance document creation, data analysis, customer service, content strategy, and countless other workflows. By learning from actual use cases, you gain not just theoretical knowledge but also the practical judgment needed to identify opportunities in your own work and implement solutions effectively.

Search Amazon.co.jp for "Generative AI Practical Applications ChatGPT" to discover the latest titles in this category.

Getting Started with ChatGPT and Claude

Books specifically focused on Claude and ChatGPT provide the hands-on introduction many beginners need. These aren't mere tool tutorials—they go deeper, exploring the reasoning behind how these models think, why they produce certain outputs, and how to structure interactions for optimal results.

Understanding Claude's particular strengths and quirks becomes valuable when you grasp the underlying principles of how it processes language and generates responses. These introductory texts bridge the gap between "I used an AI once" and "I understand how to design interactions with AI to achieve my goals." Whether you're exploring Claude for creative writing, coding assistance, analysis, or business process automation, these books establish the conceptual foundation you need.

Search Amazon.co.jp for "Claude AI Practical Guide" to find relevant introductory resources.

Prompt Engineering
