Claude Code can connect to external services through MCP servers.

⚠️
MCP servers run in your local environment. Only install from trusted sources.

Next Steps

  • Background Tasks — Integrate Claude into CI/CD via GitHub Actions
  • Custom Slash Commands — Automate project-specific workflows
  • Plugins — Combine MCP servers and skills for advanced automation

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Claude Code available on the Pro plan?

No, Claude Code requires a Max plan ($100/month or higher) or an API key. It's not available on Pro ($20/month). Individual developers can alternatively use the Claude API with pay-as-you-go pricing for Claude Code functionality.

Is the CLAUDE.md file required?

No, it's optional but highly recommended. By documenting your project's structure, tech stack, and coding conventions in CLAUDE.md, Claude Code generates more accurate and consistent code. Use /init to create one when starting a new project.

Can I use Claude Code offline?

No, Claude Code requires internet connectivity for API communication with Anthropic's servers. However, once code is generated and configuration files are created, you can edit and run them offline. The generated files remain on your local machine.

Can I create custom MCP servers?

Yes, you can build your own MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers and integrate them with Claude Code. MCP follows an open standard, allowing custom implementations. Always verify the source and security of any MCP server before installation, especially third-party ones.

en.title: Claude API Quickstart — Your First API Call in 5 Minutes
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: Get started with the Claude API. From obtaining your API key to making your first request with Python and TypeScript SDKs — a 5-minute quickstart guide.
en.content:
What is the Claude API?

The Claude API lets you integrate Claude's capabilities into your own applications. Through Anthropic's developer platform, you can programmatically access text generation, code generation, analysis, summarization, and more.

ℹ️
Using the API requires an Anthropic account and an API key. Free credits are provided, so you can start experimenting right away.

Getting Your API Key

  1. Visit console.anthropic.com
  2. Create an account or log in
  3. Navigate to the "API Keys" section and create a new key
  4. Save the key securely (it's only shown once)

Python SDK

Installation

Your First Request

Streaming Responses

For real-time output:

TypeScript SDK

Installation

Your First Request

Key Parameters

| Parameter | Description | Example | |-----------|-------------|---------| | model | Model to use | claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-6 | | max_tokens | Maximum output tokens | 1024, 4096 | | temperature | Randomness (0–1) | 0 = deterministic, 1 = creative | | system | System prompt | "You are a Python expert" | | stop_sequences | Stop strings | ["\n\n"] |

Using System Prompts

💡
System prompts are a powerful way to consistently control Claude's behavior. Use them for expert personas, standardized output formats, and domain-specific rules.

Tool Use (Function Calling)

The Claude API supports calling external functions:

Choosing the Right Model

| Model | Strengths | Best For | |-------|-----------|----------| | Claude Opus 4.6 | Highest capability, deep reasoning | Complex analysis, high-quality code | | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | Balanced speed and quality | Everyday tasks, chatbots | | Claude Haiku 4.5 | Fastest, lowest cost | High-volume processing, classification |

⚠️
API usage is billed per token. In production, manage `max_tokens` settings and rate limits carefully.

Next Steps

  • Vision (Image Input) — Analyze and describe images via the API
  • Advanced Tool Use — Automate complex multi-step workflows
  • Batch Processing — Handle large volumes of requests efficiently
  • MCP Integration — Connect services with Model Context Protocol

en.title: Automate Tasks with Cowork — Scheduled Runs and Skill-Powered Workflows
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: Learn how to automate everyday workflows with Cowork's scheduled tasks and skill system. Practical examples for file organization, report generation, and periodic checks.
en.content:
What is Cowork?

Cowork is a desktop feature of Claude designed for file operations and task automation. You can execute complex file processing and data transformations simply by giving natural language instructions — no coding required.

ℹ️
Cowork is available as part of the Claude Desktop app. It's currently in research preview.

Getting Started

In Cowork, you select a local folder and grant Claude access. Claude can then read and write files within that folder, handling a variety of tasks automatically.

What You Can Do

  • File organization — Sort files by type into folders
  • Data conversion — Transform CSV to Excel, Markdown to Word, and more
  • Report generation — Analyze data and auto-generate documents or presentations
  • Code execution — Create and run Python scripts
  • Image processing — Resize, convert formats, extract metadata

Scheduled Tasks

Cowork lets you schedule tasks to run at regular intervals using cron expressions.

Schedule Examples

| Purpose | Cron Expression | Description | |---------|-----------------|-------------| | Morning briefing | 0 9 * * 1-5 | Weekdays at 9:00 AM | | Weekly report | 0 17 * * 5 | Every Friday at 5:00 PM | | Daily backup | 0 0 * * * | Every day at midnight | | Monthly summary | 0 10 1 * * | First of each month at 10:00 AM |

💡
Cron expressions are evaluated in your local machine's timezone. No UTC conversion needed.

The Skill System

Skills extend Cowork's capabilities. Each skill bundles optimized prompts and processing steps for specific task types.

Built-in Skills

  • docx — Create and edit Word documents
  • xlsx — Work with Excel spreadsheets
  • pptx — Build PowerPoint presentations
  • pdf — Generate, merge, and split PDFs
  • frontend-design — Build frontend UI components

How Skills Work

Skills activate automatically. Say "summarize this in Excel" and the xlsx skill loads. Ask for "a presentation about Q3 results" and the pptx skill kicks in.

Practical Examples

1. Organize Your Downloads Folder

Organize files in the Downloads folder by these rules:
- PDFs → Documents/PDF/
- Images (jpg, png, gif) → Pictures/
- Excel/CSV → Documents/Data/
- Everything else → Documents/Other/

2. Analyze CSV Data into a Report

Analyze sales_data.csv and create an Excel report including:
- Monthly sales trend chart
- Top 10 products by revenue
- Year-over-year comparison table

3. Auto-Generate Weekly Email Drafts

Every Friday at 4 PM, summarize this week's work log (work_log.md)
and generate a weekly report email draft in Markdown.

Connector Integrations

Cowork connects to external services through MCP connectors:

  • Slack — Search and summarize messages
  • Asana / Jira — Task management integration
  • Google Drive — Read and write files
  • GitHub — Repository operations
⚠️
Connectors require authentication with each service. Connect them through Cowork's settings panel.

Chrome Integration

Pair Cowork with Claude in Chrome for browser automation. Collect information from websites, fill forms, take screenshots — all with natural language instructions.

Next Steps

  • Custom Skills — Define project-specific workflows
  • Multi-Connector Pipelines — Automate data flows between services
  • Advanced Scheduling — Conditional execution and error handling

en.title: Getting Started with Claude — From Sign-Up to Your First Conversation in 10 Minutes
en.author: Claude Lab
en.description: A beginner's guide to Claude. Learn how to create an account, start your first conversation, and write effective prompts in just 10 minutes.
en.content:
What is Claude?

Claude is an AI assistant developed by Anthropic. It can handle a wide range of tasks including writing, code generation, translation, summarization, and analysis.

ℹ️
Claude is available through multiple channels: the claude.ai web app, mobile apps, API, Claude Code (CLI), and Cowork (desktop).

Create Your Account

  1. Visit claude.ai
  2. Click "Sign up"
  3. Register with your email address or Google account
  4. Complete email verification

The free plan gives you access to Claude Sonnet. To use the more powerful Claude Opus, consider upgrading to the Pro plan ($20/month).

Start Your First Conversation

After logging in, you'll see the chat interface. Simply type a message in the text box to begin.

Good Prompt Example

Find and fix the bug in the following Python code.
Add a "# Fixed" comment to each change you make.

def calculate_average(numbers):
    total = 0
    for n in numbers:
        total += n
    return total / len(numbers)  # ZeroDivisionError on empty list

Tips for Effective Prompts

  • Be specific: Instead of "write something good," say "write a business email under 150 words"
  • Provide context: Share background info, target audience, and purpose
  • Specify output format: "As bullet points," "in a table," "as JSON," etc.
  • Encourage step-by-step thinking: Add "think through this step by step"
💡
Claude can understand long contexts. Pasting entire documents or reference materials and asking questions about them is an effective approach.

Plan Comparison

| Plan | Model | Price | Features | |------|-------|-------|----------| | Free | Sonnet | Free | Basic conversations, usage limits | | Pro | Opus / Sonnet | $20/mo | High-performance models, priority access | | Team | Opus / Sonnet | $25/mo/user | Team management, shared workspace | | Enterprise | All models | Contact us | SSO, audit logs, SLA |

Next Steps

Now that you know the basics, explore these topics:

  • Prompt Engineering — Techniques to dramatically improve Claude's response quality
  • Claude Code — Code directly from your terminal with Claude
  • Cowork — Desktop file operations and task automation
  • API Integration — Embed Claude into your own applications

If you'd like to deepen your understanding of AI and LLMs, check out our recommended books curated for all skill levels.
